search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae fever children Issue 2 virtual assessment referral red flags
candidate_sources_rejected: antipyretic, antibiotic, hydration, feeding and label-based dosing advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The source of fever remains clinician-assessed.
No medicine, dose, treatment or advice is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children with fever; neonates, immunocompromised children and children with complex conditions require urgent age- and condition-specific review.
setting_applicability: DHA virtual pediatric assessment partially transfers to UAE ED documentation.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA evidence; UAE pediatric emergency, infection, sepsis, antimicrobial, laboratory and referral pathways apply.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact source was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA pediatric fever section supports onset, severity or impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, caregiver concerns, risk context, appearance and vital data only when assessed; it does not infer the cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports documenting time-critical symptoms, escalation, specialist discussion and handover only when assessed or clinician-decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Pediatric virtual-care scope and age applicability were reviewed; in-person examination, tests and treatment remain manual.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact age, measured temperature, pattern, hydration, feeding, urine output, rash, breathing, neurological status, source examination, tests, diagnosis, treatment, dose, reassessment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
